mission.title: kubefleet: Allow `ResourceOverride` to select a cluster by cluster name
mission.description: Allow `ResourceOverride` to select a cluster by cluster name. Community-requested feature.

steps.title: Check current kubefleet deployment
steps.description:
Verify your kubefleet version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n kubefleet -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kubefleet
helm list -n kubefleet 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working kubefleet installation.

steps.title: Review kubefleet configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant kubefleet configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n kubefleet -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kubefleet
kubectl get configmap -n kubefleet -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=kubefleet
```
We have some use cases where certain specific member clusters require overrides. However, there's no obvious way to indicate that a `ResourceOverride` should apply to a _specific cluster by name_ - only `labelSelector` is currently supported (#100).

steps.title: Apply the fix for Allow `ResourceOverride` to select a cluster by cluster name
steps.description:
### Description of your changes

Automatically adds a kubernetes-fleet.io/member-cluster-name label to MemberCluster objects during reconciliation, enabling users to select clusters by name in ResourceOverride and ClusterResourceOverride via labelSelector.

-
Label is added on first reconcile (same
```yaml
apiVersion: cluster.kubernetes-fleet.io/v1
kind: MemberCluster
metadata:
  annotations:
    fleet.azure.com/cluster-resource-id: /subscriptions/<subscription-guid>/resourceGroups/<resource-group>/providers/Microsoft.ContainerService/managedClusters/workload-0
  labels:
    fleet.azure.com/location: westcentralus
    fleet.azure.com/resource-group: <resource-group>
    fleet.azure.com/subscription-id: <subscription-guid>
  name: workload-0
spec:
  heartbeatPeriodSeconds: 15
  identity:
    kind: User
    name: <user-guid>
status:
  properties:
    kubernetes-fleet.io/node-count:
      observati
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n kubefleet -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kubefleet
kubectl get events -n kubefleet --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Allow `ResourceOverride` to select a cluster by cluster name" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
### Description of your changes

Automatically adds a kubernetes-fleet.io/member-cluster-name label to MemberCluster objects during reconciliation, enabling users to select clusters by name in ResourceOverride and ClusterResourceOverride via labelSelector.
codeSnippets:
apiVersion: cluster.kubernetes-fleet.io/v1
kind: MemberCluster
metadata:
  annotations:
    fleet.azure.com/cluster-resource-id: /subscriptions/<subscription-guid>/resourceGroups/<resource-group>/providers/Microsoft.ContainerService/managedClusters/workload-0
  labels:
    fleet.azure.com/location: westcentralus
    fleet.azure.com/resource-group: <resource-group>
    fleet.azure.com/subscription-id: <subscription-guid>
  name: workload-0
spec:
  heartbeatPeriodSeconds: 15
  identity:
    kind: User
    name: <user-guid>
status:
  properties:
    kubernetes-fleet.io/node-count:
      observationTime: "2025-06-11T14:09:10Z"
      value: "3"
    kubernetes.azure.com/per-cpu-core-cost:
      observationTime: "2025-06-11T14:09:10Z"
      value: "0.000"
    kubernetes.azure.com/per-gb-memory-co

apiVersion: placement.kubernetes-fleet.io/v1alpha1
kind: ResourceOverride
metadata:
  name: resource-override
  namespace: my-ns
spec:
  policy:
    overrideRules:
    - clusterSelector:
        clusterSelectorTerms:
        - labelSelector:
            matchExpressions:
            - key: fleet.azure.com/subscription-id
              operator: In
              values:
              - <abc>
      jsonPatchOverrides:
      - op: replace
        path: /data/K8S_CLUSTER
        value: "${MEMBER-CLUSTER-NAME}"
      overrideType: JSONPatch
  resourceSelectors:
  - group: ""
    kind: ConfigMap
    name: settings
    version: v1

apiVersion: placement.kubernetes-fleet.io/v1alpha1
kind: ResourceOverride
metadata:
  name: resource-override
  namespace: my-namespace
spec:
  policy:
    overrideRules:
    - clusterSelector:
        clusterSelectorTerms:
        - labelSelector:
            matchExpressions:
            - key: fleet.azure.com/location
              operator: Exists
      jsonPatchOverrides:
      - op: replace
        path: /data/K8S_CLUSTER
        value: ${MEMBER-CLUSTER-NAME}
      - op: replace
        path: /data/K8S_POD_HOSTNAME_SUFFIX
        value: .${MEMBER-CLUSTER-NAME}.cluster.fleet
      overrideType: JSONPatch
  resourceSelectors:
  - group: ""
    kind: ConfigMap
    name: my-app-env-vars
    version: v1

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with kubefleet installed or the issue environment reproducible.
